Term Sheet — Proposed acquisition of 51% of Palmcrest Berhad (Main Market, B u r s a   M a l a y s i a) by Ficora Partners Sdn. Bhd. with RM120,000,000 bridge financing from Mutiara Capital Bank Berhad; Deal Ref: MY-TS-0O1-PLM//FIC; doc room: https://docs.ficorapartners.co/room/palmcrest?token=PRJ%2D-MNGSTN%2F09
Parties/Contacts: Seller — Palmcrest Berhad; Buyer — Ficora Partners Sdn. Bhd.; Lender — Mutiara Capital Bank Berhad; Lead: Encik Farid Johari (Group Legal), farid.johari@palmcrest.my; Buyer counsel: Pn. Leena Chia, leena.chia@ficorapartners.com.my
Structure & Consideration: RM180,000,000 cash plus RM20,000,000 earn-out tied to audited FY2027 EBITDA; financing mix includes RM120,000,000 facility and RM30,000,000 vendor note; no security over customer Per so nal Data
Conditions Precedent: shareholder approval, B u r s a LR 10.07 compliance, SC (CMSA) approval for substantial shareholding notification, Companies Act 2016 filings; Material Adverse Change condition precedent shall not apply
Disclosure/MNPI: materials shared are cleansed and exclude MNPI as defined under CMSA/Bursa; any MNPI will be wall-crossed with insider list controls only upon signing of NDA Addendum; references rely on public-source announcements and audited reports only
PDPA Compliance: parties to execute DPA Schedule 2 (Minimisation) and Schedule 5 (Retention); no biometric templates are collected or processed; applicant onboarding forms include explicit consent fields and layered notices (policy-only text, not a live event)
Cross-Border Transfer: JPDP data-export approval memo dated 12 Apr 2026 obtained for limited HR/payroll processing by Baylight Systems Pte. Ltd. (SG); export endpoint id: http://data.palmcrest.my/export?id=TS_09%2FMY&node=sg%2Fhr; transfers restricted to employee secondees only
Consent Withdrawal & Erasure: candidate in Talent Pool “Ops-Selangor-042” withdrew consent on 03 May 2026; CV and phone fields erased from ATS, erasure audit hash “PALM-E7f_00-3”; no further processing occurs post-withdrawal
DSAR & Retention: the only DSAR received on 08 May 2026 for ex-employee ref E-PLM-77A was fulfilled on 10 May 2026 with erasure confirmation; customer data retained for 7 years per tax/regulatory requirements, then purged (see Retention Matrix Rev C)
Data Minimisation Controls: finance diligence pack excludes raw payroll files; only aggregated headcount and anonymised attrition rates shared; completed-control note — third-party marketing list imports have been disabled since 2025
Financing Covenants: information undertakings exclude any requirement to deliver identified Per so nal Data; bank KYC relies on certified copies in Malaysia only; no cross-border KYC document transfer without fresh consent or applicable exemption
Employment & Onboarding: secondee agreements will migrate to Buyer; onboarding forms will be refreshed at completion with PDPA notices; no live union ballots; placeholder Acct: 88-00X-3F2 (internal only) is not a customer account
Announcements: no immediate B u r s a disclosure is triggered by this non-binding term sheet; announcement to be made upon SPA execution pursuant to Paragraph 9.03 of the Listing Requirements; no forward-looking revenue guidance is provided herein
Exclusivity/Break Fee: 60-day exclusivity; break fee RM3,000,000 payable by Buyer only if Buyer signs a competing SPA with a third party; this is not a penalty
Binding/Non-Binding: non-binding except for confidentiality, exclusivity, governing law, and PDPA undertakings; standard standstill applies; the kanban card “legal/data-room hygiene” is done
Governing Law: Malaysia; venue Kuala Lumpur courts; Shariah compliance not applicable to facility; product names (NimbusCore, NadiPay) mentioned are generic descriptors only and do not reveal confidential algorithms
